name: Yerba Madre
tagline: Organic yerba mate, brewed to regenerate the land
seoDescription: Yerba Madre (formerly Guayakí) is a Sebastopol-based, certified B Corp brewing organic, fair-trade yerba mate from the Atlantic Forest. Get lifted. Stay lifted.

images.heroAlt: Sharing a gourd of yerba mate at sunset

sections.heading: Get lifted. Stay lifted.
sections.subheading: Organic, fair-trade yerba mate brewed from the naturally caffeinated leaves of the South American mate tree — the clean lift behind a thriving movement.

image.alt: Sharing a gourd of yerba mate at sunset

sections.heading: A mate movement, rooted in regeneration
body: We began our journey in the mid-90s by sharing yerba mate culture across the USA. Brewed from the naturally caffeinated leaves of the mate tree native to South America, it's the clean, sustained lift that has fueled our community ever since.

For us, regeneration begins with the land. As a certified B Corp and registered Social Purpose Corporation, we source certified regenerative, organic, and fair-trade ingredients — and we've committed to planting 300,000 native trees across the Americas, starting in the Atlantic Forest where our yerba mate grows.
highlights: Certified B Corp & Social Purpose Corporation
300,000 native trees pledged across the Americas
Yerba mate sourced from the Atlantic Forest

image.alt: The founders in the early days, among bales of yerba mate

sections.heading: Brewed and canned, ready to go

items.title: Sparkling Yerba Mate
items.description: Lightly carbonated cans in bright flavors like Grapefruit Ginger and Tropical Uprising — an easy, refreshing way to get lifted.

items.title: Non-Carbonated Cans
items.description: Smooth, brewed yerba mate in flavors like Mango Fuego and Orange Exotic, for a clean lift without the fizz.

items.title: Loose-Leaf & Bagged Mate
items.description: Organic dried yerba mate for the traditional gourd-and-bombilla ritual or your own French press.

items.title: The Mate Ritual
items.description: More than a drink — a shared gourd, passed between friends. Naturally caffeinated, the way it's been enjoyed for generations.

sections.heading: Better for people and the planet

items.title: Certified B Corp
items.body: Held to verified standards of social and environmental performance — not just a label on the can.

items.title: Regenerative & organic
items.body: Certified regenerative, organic, and fair-trade ingredients, sourced to leave the land better than we found it.

items.title: 300,000 trees pledged
items.body: A commitment to plant native trees across the Americas, starting in the Atlantic Forest where our mate grows.

items.title: The clean lift
items.body: Naturally caffeinated yerba mate for a smooth, sustained energy — get lifted, stay lifted.

Yes. As a certified B Corp, we source certified regenerative, organic, and fair-trade ingredients that are better for people and the planet.

items.q: Where can I find Yerba Madre?
items.a: We're available online and in stores. Reach out through the contact page and we'll point you to the nearest spot.
We've committed to planting 300,000 native trees across the Americas, beginning in the Atlantic Forest where our yerba mate is grown.

sections.heading: Celebrating Mother Earth, one gourd at a time
sections.subheading: What started in the mid-90s as a way to share yerba mate culture across the USA has grown into a regeneration-first movement.

sections.heading: A mate movement, rooted in regeneration
body: We began our journey in the mid-90s by sharing yerba mate culture across the USA. As a certified B Corp and registered Social Purpose Corporation, we continue to promote values of community, friendship, service, and environmental stewardship. And we're only getting started.

For us, regeneration begins with the land. That's why we've committed to planting 300,000 native trees across South and North America, starting in the Atlantic Forest, where our yerba mate grows, and extending to the regions where we operate in the U.S. With Yerba Madre we celebrate Mother Earth, the life-giving power of the mate plant, and our role as madre to a thriving yerba mate movement.
highlights: Certified B Corp & Social Purpose Corporation
300,000 native trees pledged across the Americas
Yerba mate sourced from the Atlantic Forest

flags: Category from lead is "cafe" but Yerba Madre is a packaged yerba-mate BEVERAGE BRAND (formerly Guayakí), a B Corp HQ'd in Sebastopol — not a sit-down cafe. Site authored as a beverage brand; confirm framing before sending.
Established 1996 read from the product can ("EST. 1996") + the site's "mid-90s" copy — verify exact founding year before asserting.
No phone, email, or street address scraped — add a real contact method or form before sending.
No hours (national CPG brand, not a storefront) — contact page hides the hours/map block intentionally; confirm.
No genuine customer reviews scraped — testimonials section omitted rather than fabricated.
Several gallery/lifestyle photos show the older GUAYAKÍ can branding (same company, pre-rebrand) — fine, but confirm the brand is comfortable showing both.
